title: Pediatric fatigue
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Specifies fatigue description, onset, course, duration, functional effect, sleep, mood, associated symptoms, medication, family, and social history.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Fatigue – 06
source.population: People presenting with fatigue; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth fatigue documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines fatigue domains, duration categories, and broad medical, psychological, medication, and substance-related associations.

assessment.text: Defines fatigue domains, duration categories, and broad medical, psychological, medication, and substance-related associations.
assessment.rationale: Supports fatigue duration and medical, psychological, medicine and substance associations for child fatigue; no decision is generated.

assessment.text: Lists weight loss, bleeding, breathlessness, fever, neurological progression, malignancy features, and suicidal tendency as concern prompts.
assessment.rationale: Supports systemic, neurological and self-harm warnings for child fatigue; no decision is generated.
